container_template.readme:
# Gemma 4 31B (Unsloth GGUF) with llama.cpp

This deployment runs the `Gemma 4 31B IT` GGUF model with the official `llama.cpp` CUDA server and exposes an OpenAI-compatible API for tools such as OpenClaw, OpenCode, and other compatible clients.

<Callout variation="note">This recipe is public by default. If you enabled Container Gateway Authentication during deployment, include `Salad-Api-Key` in your requests.</Callout>

## Use With OpenClaw

If you want to connect this deployment to OpenClaw or other agentic tools, follow our guides:

- [Use Agentic Tools with a Salad-hosted LLM](https://docs.salad.com/container-engine/tutorials/agentic-tools)

## Access

- <Link url={`https://${props.networking.dns}`}>Open the built-in llama.cpp UI</Link>
- <Link url={`https://${props.networking.dns}/health`}>Health Check</Link>
- <Link url={`https://${props.networking.dns}/v1/models`}>List Models</Link>

## Defaults

- Model source: `unsloth/gemma-4-31B-it-GGUF`
- Model file: `gemma-4-31B-it-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf`
- API model name: `gemma-4-31b-it`
- Context length: `256K`
- GPU offload: `auto`
- Parallel slots: `1`
- KV cache types: `q8_0 / q8_0`
- Sampling defaults: `temperature 1.0`, `top_p 0.95`, `min_p 0.0`, `top_k 64`
- Public access: enabled by default unless you turned auth on during deployment

This recipe uses the Unsloth GGUF conversion of Gemma 4 31B.

This recipe is tuned for long-context text workloads on a single GPU.

## Thinking Mode

To explicitly request reasoning, start your system prompt with `<|think|>`.

For example:

```json
{"role": "system", "content": "<|think|> You are a careful reasoning assistant."}
```

The startup sampling values are defaults. They can still be overridden per request with `temperature`, `top_p`, and `min_p` in your inference payload.

## Curl Example

<Callout variation="note">Omit the `Salad-Api-Key` header if you did not enable auth.</Callout>

<CodeBlock language="bash">{`curl https://${props.networking.dns}/v1/chat/completions \\
  -X POST \\
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\
  -H 'Salad-Api-Key: ${props.apiKey}' \\
  -d '{
    "model": "gemma-4-31b-it",
    "messages": [
      {"role": "system", "content": "<|think|> You are a careful reasoning assistant."},
      {"role": "user", "content": "Summarize why long context can help coding agents."}
    ],
    "max_tokens": 512
  }'
`}</CodeBlock>

## Resources

- [Unsloth Gemma 4 31B GGUF repo](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/gemma-4-31B-it-GGUF)
- [llama.cpp server docs](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/tree/master/tools/server)
- [Recipe Source](https://github.com/SaladTechnologies/salad-recipes/tree/master/recipes/gemma-4-31b-it-llama-cpp)

form.title: Gemma 4 31B IT (llama.cpp)
form.description:
Run `Gemma 4 31B IT` with `llama.cpp` as an OpenAI-compatible API for a wide variety of tools.

This recipe is designed to be easy for nontechnical users:

- the model is already chosen for you
- it downloads automatically on startup
- it is tuned for a single GPU
- it is optimized for long-context text chat, coding, and agent tools such as OpenClaw and OpenCode

If you want the simple version: enter a container group name, decide whether you want public or private access, deploy, and wait for startup to finish.

If you want more technical detail, read below.

<Callout variation="note">The model is downloaded at startup and can take several minutes before the deployment becomes ready.</Callout>

What is already configured for you:

- Unsloth's `unsloth/gemma-4-31B-it-GGUF` GGUF conversion of the Google Gemma 4 31B instruction model
- the `UD-Q4_K_XL` GGUF file, which is a practical single-GPU choice for this model size
- full `256K` context length
- automatic GPU layer offload
- Flash Attention and fit-to-memory tuning
- `q8_0` key/value cache types to make long context more practical on a single GPU
- public access by default, with optional Container Gateway Authentication in the form

This recipe is intentionally optimized for text chat, coding, and agent workflows. Gemma 4 is multimodal, but this recipe stays text-first by default to preserve memory headroom for the long context window on consumer hardware.

Reasoning is controlled per request. To turn reasoning on, start the system prompt with `<|think|>`. To leave reasoning off, do not include that token. 

For technical users: if you want to change defaults, check advanced settings.

Helpful references:

- [NVIDIA Gemma-4-31B-IT-NVFP4 model card](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Gemma-4-31B-IT-NVFP4)
- [Unsloth Gemma 4 31B GGUF repo](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/gemma-4-31B-it-GGUF)
- [llama.cpp server docs](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/tree/master/tools/server)
- [NVIDIA Gemma 4 deployment overview](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/bringing-ai-closer-to-the-edge-and-on-device-with-gemma-4/)

container_group_name.title: Container Group Name
container_group_name.description: Required* Must be 2-63 lowercase letters, numbers, or hyphens. Cannot start with a number or start or end with a hyphen.

networking_auth.title: Require Container Gateway Authentication
networking_auth.description: When enabled, requests must include a SaladCloud API Key to access the container. When disabled, public (unauthenticated) access is allowed.

short_description: Serve Gemma 4 31B with llama.cpp as an OpenAI-compatible API for OpenClaw, OpenCode, and other tools.
